codeReview.summary: The commit delivers solid size-variant-aware packing, PACKING_RECORD reference linkage, cost traceability DTOs, V38 migration with backfill, and packaging material type enforcement. Accounting entries correctly go through AccountingFacade. Gate-fast passes (380 tests, 0 failures). However, a core requirement — consolidating PackingService and BulkPackingService into one clear service — was NOT addressed. BulkPackingService (788 lines) still exists as a separate service with overlapping packing logic. PackingService itself grew to 994 lines (nearly 2x the 500-line convention). Frontend handoff documentation was not updated with the new size-variant/traceability changes.

issues.description: Feature requirement #5 explicitly says 'Clean up duplicate/overlapping logic between PackingService and BulkPackingService -- consolidate into one clear service.' BulkPackingService still exists at 788 lines with its own packing logic and AccountingFacade.postPackingJournal() calls. The expectedBehavior 'No duplicate packing services' is not met.

PackingService is 994 lines, nearly 2x the 500-line service limit from AGENTS.md coding conventions. The size-variant resolution, child FG matching, journal linkage, and semi-finished consumption logic could be extracted into focused helper services (e.g., PackingJournalService, PackingCostService).

Frontend handoff doc was NOT updated in this commit. New DTOs (CostComponentTraceDto, PackedBatchTraceDto, RawMaterialTraceDto), size variant fields on PackingRecordDto/ProductionLogPackingRecordDto, and the SizeVariant CRUD/auto-generation behavior are undocumented for frontend consumption. AGENTS.md and skill mandate handoff doc updates for any API-touching feature.

isMatchingChildSku() uses prefix matching (startsWith parentSku + '-') to validate child FG SKU family membership. This is fragile — a product 'PAINT' would match 'PAINT-RED' but also 'PAINTER-KIT'. Consider using a more robust relationship (e.g., product_id FK on finished_good or explicit parent-child link) rather than string prefix matching.

CatalogService grew to 639 lines (above 500-line limit). The new size-variant auto-generation on product upsert (parseSizeToLiters, createOrUpdateSizeVariant) adds ~80 lines of logic that could live in a dedicated SizeVariantService.

isLikelyPackagingMaterial() uses heuristic string matching on product name/SKU/category to determine if an imported raw material should have MaterialType.PACKAGING. This could lead to false positives (e.g., a product named 'Bucket Paint' being misclassified). Consider making this explicit via a CSV column or category mapping rather than string heuristics.

sharedStateObservations.observation: PackingService at 994 lines and BulkPackingService at 788 lines both exceed the 500-line service limit documented in AGENTS.md. The feature was supposed to consolidate these into one but did not. AGENTS.md should perhaps note the BulkPackingService consolidation as a known tech debt item if this review doesn't block.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: wc -l PackingService.java = 994, BulkPackingService.java = 788. AGENTS.md states 'No service class should exceed ~500 lines'.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker deviated from TDD (Step 2) and mission context file discovery (Step 1.1). The worker acknowledged both deviations in skillFeedback. The skill's Step 1 assumes mission context files are at a specific path but doesn't specify the path — the worker spent time discovering they were absent in the worker environment.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: handoff.skillFeedback.followedProcedure=false. Deviations: (1) 'Worked from an in-progress feature branch with substantial pre-existing staged changes' instead of TDD. (2) 'Attempted to locate mission.md/AGENTS.md/features.json in expected locations... were not present at the expected repository paths'.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The feature uses postPackingJournal() on AccountingFacade (a specialized method) alongside createStandardJournal() (the standardized method). AGENTS.md says 'ALL journal creation MUST go through createStandardJournal(JournalCreationRequest)' but postPackingJournal is a pre-existing specialized facade method. This inconsistency should be documented or the specialized method should be deprecated in favor of createStandardJournal.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: PackingService.java:571 and :729 use accountingFacade.postPackingJournal(). PackingService.java:808 uses accountingFacade.createStandardJournal(). AGENTS.md service design rules say all journal creation must use createStandardJournal.

summary: FAIL — The core requirement to consolidate PackingService and BulkPackingService into a single clear service was not addressed. BulkPackingService (788 lines) still exists alongside PackingService (994 lines) with overlapping packing/journal logic. This directly violates the feature's expectedBehavior 'No duplicate packing services'. The implementation otherwise delivers strong work: SizeVariant entity with proper migration/backfill, packaging material type enforcement, cost traceability DTOs, PACKING_RECORD reference linkage for journal/movement traceability, and comprehensive test updates (380 gate-fast tests passing). Non-blocking issues include missing frontend handoff doc updates, service size exceeding conventions, and fragile SKU prefix matching for child FG validation.
